name: per-rule canary: every state transition appends an entry {timestamp, from, to, reason} to the rule's rule_state_history
command: mkdir -p rubric/fp-log && : > rubric/fp-log/g-x-001.jsonl && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/canary-promote.sh" --rule-id g-x-001 --to block --tree "$PWD/tree" --now 2026-05-13T00:00:00Z && grep -E "from: warn-only.*to: block" tree/google/x.yaml 1>&2 && echo ok 1>&2
setup:
mkdir -p tree/google && printf 'source:
  id: g
  authoritative_publisher: G
  authoritative_url: https://g.com
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: 2026-05-13T00:00:00Z
  content_hash: sha256:S
  fetch_frequency: daily
  fragility_tier: low
  license_note: MIT
rules:
  - {id: g-x-001, name: x, description: x, detector: x, type: problem, fixable: null, has_suggestions: false, deprecated: false, replaced_by: [], docs_url: https://x.com, requires_type_checking: false, recommended: true, options_schema: {}, messages: {default: x}, severity: P1, version: 1.0.0, semver: 1.0.0, rule_state: warn-only, rule_state_history: [{timestamp: 2026-04-28T00:00:00Z, from: candidate, to: warn-only, reason: initial-promotion}], provenance: [{source: x, class: published-standard, section_id: x, url: https://x.com, fetched_at: 2026-05-13T00:00:00Z, content_hash: sha256:S}]}
recommended_set: [g-x-001]
all_set: [g-x-001]
' > tree/google/x.yaml
